entities.name: Snap

evidence.quote: I feel like Snap has always been punching above its weight in terms of just how much new stuff comes out of your team, stories, AR glasses, swipe-based navigation.

evidence.quote: You think about a lot of the big consumer technology companies today, they were really born out of mobile, whether it's Uber, Snapchat, some of those early generation companies that really got distribution and built themselves on mobile.

entities.name: 分销 (distribution)
entities.how_described: 本集核心概念之一。嘉宾和主持人都认为，人们过度沉迷于做产品而没花足够时间思考分发；在 AI 逐渐接管代码和战略的漏斗后，分发正成为新计算平台前新的护城河和最大难题。

evidence.quote: People don't spend nearly enough time thinking about distribution and figuring out distribution.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Well, I think it's really interesting in terms of the examples that you just shared of TikTok and threads, because you just shared two examples of people who figured out distribution.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: It's a lot harder to get distribution for new ideas and new services.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And both TikTok and Threads figured out distribution, which is why I think there are more recent examples of success.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And I think with Threads, obviously, they were able to leverage the amazing distribution that Meta has across all of their other products.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And I think people don't spend nearly enough time thinking about distribution and figuring out distribution.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: So as I reflect on the early days of building Snapchat, the thing that we figured out in terms of distribution, especially when it comes to social, was that back then people believed that there were network effects and social networks that meant that the more people you had using the service, the stickier that it is.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: We had the benefit of the app store and mobile distribution, but we also had a different way of growing our service, which wasn't about having the most friends.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: It's something that I've been thinking a lot about actually, this idea of distribution, because of AI.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And I feel like distribution is where it ends up being, what ends up being the new moat and the new biggest challenge because AI is not going to really help you there.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: You think about a lot of the big consumer technology companies today, they were really born out of mobile, whether it's Uber, Snapchat, some of those early generation companies that really got distribution and built themselves on mobile.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Especially if they have distribution already.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: It's going to be very hard to get distribution just like there's so much launching every day, so much to pay attention to.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 护城河 (moat)
entities.how_described: 本集反复讨论的战略概念。嘉宾指出软件不是护城河，只靠网络效应也不够，必须叠加构建极难复制的创作者生态与垂直整合的硬件堆栈，才能建立真正的防御性业务。

evidence.quote: 15 years ago, we essentially learned that software is not a moat, which is something that everyone is discovering today with AI.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And I feel like distribution is where it ends up being, what ends up being the new moat and the new biggest challenge because AI is not going to really help you there.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And so I think one of the things, it's been an interesting time for me to reflect a lot on our strategy over the last 15 years, because 15 years ago, we essentially learned that software is not a moat, which is something that everyone is discovering today with AI.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: You would think network effects would be the moat.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: That's what everybody's always trying to get to because that's classically a great moat.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Are you saying that that's maybe not even as great a moat or that's just not enough and then you need something more like say hardware?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 网络效应 (network effects)
entities.how_described: 传统认为社交网络靠它形成垄断（越多人用越有价值），但嘉宾认为它只是拼图里重要的一块，不足以抵抗软件克隆，必须要在其之上叠加生态和硬件才能形成有效护城河。

evidence.quote: So as I reflect on the early days of building Snapchat, the thing that we figured out in terms of distribution, especially when it comes to social, was that back then people believed that there were network effects and social networks that meant that the more people you had using the service, the stickier that it is.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: You would think network effects would be the moat.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I think network effects are certainly an important piece of the puzzle for sure, but I don't think they're enough when it comes to these sorts of software cloning.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And so I think in addition to network effects, it's really important to try to build businesses that are more defensible by truly building out a platform that can support relationships between creators, developers, and the community, because that in and of itself we found is incredibly difficult to copy.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: 智能体 (agent)
entities.how_described: 本集指用来执行闭环任务的 AI 程序。Snap 围绕用户旅程的具体任务（jobs to be done）构建了 go-to-market 智能体，能一次性自动跑完编写规格文档、风险分析、找审批人、写推广材料等完整工作流。

evidence.quote: And I've built just an agent that will go and comb through everything that's happening in the company and let me know what's up, what I need to focus on, what I need to catch.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And that agent was, you built it in Glean.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I think one of the things that we're thinking a lot about is just taking the entire workflow and enabling it with an agent.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: So for example, taking a product idea and with our go to-market agent, taking that product idea, writing the spec, identifying the relevant folks who need to be involved in sign-offs and understanding it, actually helping to do the risk analysis on the product from a legal, trust and safety perspective, writing the go to-market materials like the blog, et cetera.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: So if we can define a job to be done clearly enough that we can build an agent to do it, that can really create a lot of lift.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Where do you build this agent?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 增强现实 (augmented reality)
entities.how_described: Snap 押注的下一代计算形态。嘉宾认为在巴掌大的手机屏上做 AR 像通过钥匙孔看世界，而通过 AR 眼镜将内容锚定在真实物理空间里，能把人带到户外并与朋友产生互动。

evidence.quote: One of the first things we did was focus a lot on building ecosystems, whether those are the relationships between creators and Snap-chatters, whether that's the platform we built around augmented reality where developers have built millions of these lenses.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And then it also informed a lot of our thinking about investing in other places that are really hard to copy, including hardware, where it's really, really challenging to copy our fully vertically integrated stack around augmented reality.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: We were innovating, building lenses and augmented reality, but ultimately we saw that people were using AR on these tiny little phone screens that required you to use your thumbs to interact.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: We have the biggest augmented reality platform.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: 待办任务 (jobs to be done)
entities.how_described: Snap 进行全公司 AI 改造的底层机制。把用户和广告商旅程拆解成极其具体的任务清单，只要任务能被清晰定义，就可以围绕它组建跨职能团队并用 AI 工具和智能体跑通整个闭环工作流。

evidence.quote: And so the way that we did that was really by starting with the jobs to be done for our community and our advertisers.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: So getting really, really basic and straightforward on, okay, for Snap-chatters, our jobs to be done, it's as simple as like get people to download the app, get them to add their close friends, get them to, I don't know, use lenses, something like that.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And so by listing out all these jobs to be done, really for the community journey and for advertisers as well, it became very clear where we could use agents, where we needed to be very focused in terms of building cross-functional teams around those jobs supported by AI tools.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And that's connected to how we think about those jobs to be done.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: Spectacles
entities.how_described: Snap 投入长达 12 年的自研 AR 眼镜产品线。本集提到它第一代旨在把摄像头从口袋里拿出来，而 2024 年发布的第五代 Specs 已搭载自研操作系统并允许开发者构建全功能软件，为正式面向消费者发布做准备。

evidence.quote: You guys launched spectacles before Meta got into this stuff.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: You had spectacles, you had drones.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Is it specs or spectacles the name of what's coming out today, by the way?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So with the early version of Spectacles, I guess that was back in 2014 or something like that, the initial idea was just to get the camera off the phone out of your pocket and allow you to more easily take snaps out in the world.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: Specs
entities.how_described: 最新一代的 Spectacles 眼镜，将在今年正式发布。嘉宾称它为继 iPhone 之后等了 20 年终于该来的新计算形态，能把内容直接放在视野中而不需要低头看小屏幕。

evidence.quote: And then you have specs coming out.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Is it specs or spectacles the name of what's coming out today, by the way?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Specs is coming out this year.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Specs.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And then with the last generation in 2024, we released our operating system, which now allows developers to build full featured software inside of specs in preparation for the consumer launch.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: So I got an early preview of the specs and it's extremely cool.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I do think social norms will play a big role, but I also think one of the things people experience with AR, specs, unlike these heads up display glasses, they don't just put an annoying little screen that's stuck to your face on the glasses.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: I think what is going to be really important with specs is showing people all of the incredible new ways they can hang out and connect together with their friends wearing specs, but I don't think it'll be disruptive or notification based or something like that.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: But I think because I really like leading our company and leading the direction and strategy for the business, and because I see a huge opportunity in transforming the way that people use computing in the future, both through Snapchat, but also with specs, I think this is the right role for me right now.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: It's about to launch specs after 12 years of investment in this future computing platform, but at the same time, it's still not net income profitable, for example, because we've been investing so heavily in the future and making the choice to do that.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And the reason why that's so important is that it's going to be very hard for us to win long-term in specs without a really solid foundation.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And I think one of the things that will play an important role in that is getting specs out to consumers because I think right now we've talked so long about the role that specs will play in the world.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Having built Snapchat and then also specs, just how do you think about devices and your kids?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Our beta testers for specs, which is really fun.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: stories
entities.how_described: Snap 发明并被全行业模仿的经典功能。本集复盘了其设计逻辑：通过倾听用户对压力和按倒序排生日派对的抱怨，Snap 创造了满足本质需求且按时间顺序排、24小时后消失的新解法，而非字面照做群发按钮。

evidence.quote: I feel like Snap has always been punching above its weight in terms of just how much new stuff comes out of your team, stories, AR glasses, swipe-based navigation.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And over the years, Evan and his team have invented the concept of stories.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And just like a few examples that come to mind, stories you guys created.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Well, let's use stories as an example, right?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: So we listened to all of that and heard all of that, but then we came up with something totally new and different, which were stories that were responsive to the feedback.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And they, maybe most importantly, were in chronological order, which is the way that people have told stories since the beginning of time.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: I love hearing these stories of features that everyone's using now and how they came to be.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And then again, through talking to people and iterating, understanding what folks were really looking for, we ended up with stories and some of the gestures that people maybe take for granted today, being able to tap forward through stories and backwards through stories, full screen, vertical content.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: These stories are so interesting and people don't know these histories.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: One of my favorite stories was around screenshot detection actually in the early days because when we first built Snapchat and we shared with people, "Oh, you could send photos that disappear."
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: I would recommend the first half because he interviews like 150 early Apple team members and the stories are just great and there's a lot of learnings in there.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: Snapchat plus
entities.how_described: Snap 的订阅服务。本集提到它刚刚达到 2500 万订阅、营收年化破 10 亿美元，但其模式与命名立刻被竞争对手 Meta 原样照搬成了 Instagram+。

evidence.quote: Yeah, I think we just hit 25 million subscribers on Snapchat plus more than a billion revenue run rate.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: One of my favorite stories was around screenshot detection actually in the early days because when we first built Snapchat and we shared with people, "Oh, you could send photos that disappear."
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: 自动代码审查 (automated code review)
entities.how_described: Snap 为防范上十亿用户规模下的代码风险而引入的 AI 护栏机制。目前已自动检测出接近一万个 Bug，让设计师也能安全地亲自提交代码。

evidence.quote: So we've got automated code review now.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1
